strengths: TypeScript, Node.js, React, AWS CDK, and MongoDB production experience at Optilyz and re:cinq maps directly onto the primary stack and cloud/database requirements with no translation needed.

Hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq — MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks — satisfies the explicit AI agent development requirement that most senior fullstack candidates will not meet.

10+ years of Staff/Senior full-stack delivery across web, micro-services, mobile, and embedded systems, including TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and architecture contributions, demonstrates the engineering judgment and breadth the role demands.
concerns: Career gap since October 2025 (~9 months) will need context to confirm readiness to re-engage at full velocity.
Nest.js and Vitest are not explicitly named in the resume; the role lists these as primary framework choices, so familiarity will need to be confirmed.
Kotlin/Java exposure is limited to a robotics context at Altom (2016–2018); the secondary backend services requirement in those languages may require a ramp-up period.
response.justification: Bogdan brings a rare combination of deep TypeScript/Node.js/React production experience and direct AI-agent infrastructure work — MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks at re:cinq — that together satisfy the two hardest-to-find requirements on this job description. His Optilyz tenure, scaling a direct-mail automation platform to millions of annual sends with AWS CDK and MongoDB, demonstrates cloud-native architecture thinking and high-reliability delivery at exactly the seniority the role targets. A decade-plus track record at Staff and Senior levels across multiple domains, paired with consistent application of TDD, code review, and CI/CD practices, signals the engineering judgment and collaboration skills the team needs; the career gap and sparse Nest.js/Kotlin exposure are bridgeable gaps against an otherwise strong match.
